reasoning: The most recent value of the yield spread (as of 2025-05-09) is 0.03. The linear trend over the very short term is technically positive but essentially flat, and momentum has been somewhat downward in the last few days. The automated FRED tool implies a probability of 0.42 over a 7-day horizon, blending the linear trend (P=0.51) and negative seasonality/recent noise. Due to the high noise-to-signal ratio and essentially random-walk behavior of interest rates over non-macro timeframes, probability should converge strongly to 0.5 for longer horizons. Therefore, I start with a slight bias against an increase based on the short-term model and momentum, gradually sliding my estimates towards maximum uncertainty (0.50) as the forecast horizon extends, to adhere to the rule that uncertainty increases over time.
